reasoning: Both models agree the package is safe. PKGBUILD shows standard practices with verified sources. Critical files like install hooks and systemd services reviewed by qwen show legitimate hardware control functionality without malicious patterns.
coverage_issues: deepseek failed to review ANY source files due to incorrect path selection (10/10 selected files didn't exist)
Neither model reviewed the actual build/install scripts executed during makepkg (e.g., pip operations in PKGBUILD)
false_negatives: deepseek missed network/filesystem access risks by skipping all source code (e.g., USB device handling in _pyusb_find.py)
Potential privilege escalation in install-helper.sh wasn't flagged as risk (though deemed safe)
learnings: Improve file selection: Prioritize build/install hooks referenced in PKGBUILD over arbitrary source files
Fix path handling: Models must account for extracted source directories when selecting files
Enforce critical file review: Always audit files executed during makepkg (prepare/build/package functions)
Reduce hallucination: Sanitize model file selections against actual filesystem
